name: Business Readiness Assessment
description: Evaluate whether strategy, market understanding, operations, leadership, and financial foundations are ready to support execution.

overview.heading: Business Readiness Assessment
overview.description: Assess how prepared your organization is to turn priorities into consistent execution across five essential business dimensions.
overview.disclaimer: This assessment provides a directional readiness view and should be considered alongside business context and professional judgment.

stages.name: Strategic Clarity
stages.description: How clearly priorities, choices, and measures of success guide the organization.

questions.prompt: Our organization has a small number of clearly defined priorities for the next 12 months.
Teams understand how their work contributes to the organization’s strategic priorities.
Leadership uses agreed measures to review progress and adjust direction.

stages.name: Market & Customer Readiness
stages.description: Understanding of target customers, market demand, differentiation, and opportunity.

questions.prompt: We have a clear understanding of the customers or stakeholders we are best positioned to serve.
Customer feedback and market evidence regularly influence our decisions.
Our value proposition is meaningfully differentiated from available alternatives.

stages.name: Operational Readiness
stages.description: The reliability, scalability, and visibility of processes and operating systems.

questions.prompt: Critical business processes are documented, understood, and followed consistently.
Our systems and tools provide reliable information for day-to-day decisions.
The organization can handle a meaningful increase in demand without major disruption.

stages.name: Leadership & People
stages.description: Leadership alignment, accountability, capability, and the capacity of the team.

questions.prompt: Leadership communicates decisions and expectations clearly and consistently.
Responsibilities and decision ownership are clear across the organization.
We have the capabilities and leadership capacity needed for our current priorities.

stages.name: Financial & Risk Readiness
stages.description: Financial visibility, resource discipline, and preparedness for material business risks.

questions.prompt: Leadership has timely visibility of cash flow, costs, and financial performance.
Resources are allocated according to agreed priorities rather than immediate pressure alone.
Material business risks have clear owners and practical response plans.

bands.label: Strong
bands.interpretation: This area appears well established and capable of supporting confident execution.

bands.label: Established
bands.interpretation: A sound foundation is present, with selected opportunities to improve consistency or scale.

bands.label: Developing
bands.interpretation: Important elements are in place, but execution may still depend on individuals or informal practices.

bands.label: Constrained
bands.interpretation: Gaps in this area may limit execution and should be addressed through focused improvement.

bands.label: Critical
bands.interpretation: This area requires immediate attention because it may materially affect business performance or resilience.

profiles.title: Strategy Ahead of Execution
profiles.description: Strategic direction is comparatively strong, but operating foundations may not yet support reliable delivery.
profiles.recommendation: Convert strategic priorities into repeatable processes, decision rights, capacity plans, and measurable operating commitments.

profiles.title: Operationally Capable, Financially Exposed
profiles.description: Execution capability is developing, but financial visibility or risk preparedness may create avoidable exposure.
profiles.recommendation: Strengthen cash-flow visibility, resource allocation discipline, and ownership of priority risks before expanding commitments.

profiles.title: Opportunity with Internal Gaps
profiles.description: Market understanding appears promising, while internal capability may need strengthening before opportunity can be pursued consistently.
profiles.recommendation: Prioritize the people, process, and financial constraints that could prevent the organization from converting demand into sustainable results.

profiles.title: Ready to Execute
profiles.description: The organization demonstrates broad readiness across the essential foundations required to execute priorities with confidence.
profiles.recommendation: Translate readiness into a focused execution roadmap with owners, milestones, and regular performance reviews.

profiles.title: Developing Readiness
profiles.description: The business has several useful foundations, but readiness is uneven and may reduce consistency of execution.
profiles.recommendation: Select the two lowest-scoring dimensions and define a 90-day improvement plan with clear owners and evidence of progress.

profiles.title: Foundation Building
profiles.description: Core business foundations require coordinated attention before the organization can execute major priorities reliably.
profiles.recommendation: Stabilize strategic focus, operating discipline, leadership accountability, and financial visibility before adding complexity.

report.heading: Your Business Readiness Report
report.intro: This report summarizes overall readiness, highlights the dimensions supporting execution, and identifies where focused improvement may have the greatest value.
report.cta_heading: Ready to strengthen your business foundations?
report.cta_text: Share your results to discuss the priorities, constraints, and next steps identified by your assessment.
